problem:
The cubic polynomial $p(x)$ satisfies $p(2) = 1,$ $p(7) = 19,$ $p(15) = 11,$ and $p(20) = 29.$  Find
\[p(1) + p(2) + p(3) + \dots + p(21).\]

classified_questions.question: What is the given information about the cubic polynomial $p(x)$?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How can we utilize the information about $p(2),$ $p(7),$ $p(15),$ and $p(20)$ to determine other values of $p(x)$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What does it mean for the given points to form a parallelogram?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How can we represent the cubic polynomial using a new function $f(x)$?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: What is the relationship between $f(x)$ and $p(x)$?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: Why did we choose the function $g(x) = -f(-x)$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How did we apply the Identity Theorem to show that $f(x)$ and $g(x)$ are the same polynomial?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: Why did we come up with the equation $p(11 - x) + p(x + 11) = 30$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How can we use this equation to find the sum $S = p(1) + p(2) + p(3) + \dots + p(21)$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What can we conclude about the value of $S$?
classified_questions.label: b
